text:
There once roamed a peculiar squirrel named Olaf. Olaf loved nothing more than exploring the forest, collecting nuts, and sometimes...a shiny pebble. One day, Olaf discovered something utterly extraordinary. Hidden behind an ancient oak was an old book. It was partially worn out but Olaf loved old things. He fluttered his little wings and fluttered the old tome open.

Inside were tales of creatures wonderful and strange. Olaf couldnowiolkoSnotlotte? Maybe...Butterfly-O-O-Oo?

With ecstatic eyes, Olfe-o-Ooolaffiola(o?) read about these marvelous creatures. There was the Moonwort Owl, wise as old owl, and the Meadow Glimmerfrog, so clever. Olaf felt such admiration.

That night, under the moonlit oak.

Olfe...wanted one.

Thought Olfe.

Maybe…

...

The next day, Olfe wasn the wisest owl.

Butterfly?!

...

Oh wow!

He fluttered around
